body.title: Automatic per-ticket cost total (universal) + ledger domain/tier/outcome/ticket_id columns
body.description:
**Intention (Akien, verbatim):** actual cost per ticket should be tracked for EVERY single ticket, automagically — universal infrastructure, NOT something added per-case. We learn from our mistakes by watching real cost per ticket.

**Already automatic + universal (shipped in T-inference-cost-learn-verify):** every InferenceDevice.dispatch() emits a per-CALL cost_record log (ticket_id, domain, tier, source, tokens, dollars, call_outcome) keyed by ticket_id — all callers, not DS-specific. So the per-call DATA is there.

**The gap this ticket closes:** there is no automatic per-TICKET TOTAL. Today 'cost of ticket T-x' means grep the cost_record lines for that ticket_id and sum by hand. budget_summary rolls up by session/coa/instance only (no 'ticket'), and the durable budget_ledger row does not even carry ticket_id. Make the per-ticket cost total AUTOMATIC and UNIVERSAL: (1) a verified idempotent migration adding domain, tier, call_outcome, ticket_id columns (ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS) to clan.budget_ledger; (2) extend debit() to write them (thread from device.py's debit call — the fields are already on the request/cost_record); (3) budget_summary(group_by='ticket') so a ticket's total \$ is one query for EVERY ticket, every caller, automatically. Run against real Postgres and confirm no silent no-op (a bare INSERT with absent columns is swallowed by debit's except → kills ALL ledger writes invisibly — hence the ALTER must land first).

**Affected files:** budget_ledger.py (debit signature+INSERT, budget_summary group_by), device.py (debit call args — ticket_id/domain/tier/outcome), a migration script/step.
**Design rules:** NO SQLITE; Postgres only; NEVER a bare INSERT with new columns absent an applied ALTER; migration idempotent; log at the crossing.
**Scope boundary:** IN: ledger columns + verified migration + debit threading + per-ticket cost rollup query (universal). OUT: the log cost_record (shipped), dashboards/UI.
**Test plan:** integration test against real Postgres (DB rule — no mocks): a debit writes a row with domain/tier/outcome/ticket_id; budget_summary(group_by='ticket') returns the summed \$ for a ticket_id across multiple calls; an existing-schema DB still writes after the idempotent migration.
**Completion criteria:** every ticket's total inference cost is an automatic query (budget_summary group_by='ticket'), universal across all callers; ledger rows carry domain+tier+outcome+ticket_id; migration verified idempotent on a live DB; no silent no-op.
body.intention: I intend that the per-call cost+outcome record is durable and aggregatable in Postgres (cost per domain/tier over time), not only greppable in the logs — so cost learning survives log rotation.
